title: Fix compiler warnings and add unit tests for constraint derivation
description: 14 compiler warnings from unused enum variants, unused fields, etc. Fix by either wiring them through the pipeline or gating with #[allow] where they are genuinely for future use. Add unit tests for derive_constraints: test that readonly params get AllocReadonly, that Bool returns get no extra constraint, that Enum types get discriminant range preconditions.

title: CI: GitHub Actions workflow for build + test
description: Set up GitHub Actions workflow that runs on PR and push to main. Steps: cargo fmt --check, cargo clippy, cargo test, cargo build --release. Test on windows-latest and ubuntu-latest.

title: Add integration test with MSP Rust request_validator
description: End-to-end test: use mir-json output from the Rust MSP crate, feed to saw-spec-gen from-mir-json, verify generated specs have correct immutable reference constraints, correct Result/Option return type discriminant bounds, and that specs can be loaded by SAW.

title: Add integration test with MSP C++ RequestValidator
description: End-to-end test: run clang -ast-dump=json on the MSP C++ RequestValidator.cpp, feed to saw-spec-gen, verify the generated .saw specs have correct readonly annotations for const& params, correct return type (bool), and that the specs can be loaded by SAW without errors. Add test fixtures in tests/ directory.

title: Emit mir_verify specs in addition to llvm_verify specs
description: saw_emit.rs currently only generates llvm_verify-style SAW scripts (llvm_alloc, llvm_fresh_var, etc.). Add a parallel emitter for mir_verify specs using mir_alloc, mir_fresh_var, mir_points_to, mir_enum_value, mir_precond. The constraint derivation is the same — only the emitted SAW commands differ. Add a --target flag (llvm or mir) to the CLI.

title: MIR JSON: resolve struct/ADT layouts from mir-json type definitions
description: mir-json output includes ADT (algebraic data type) definitions with field names, types, and layout info (size, alignment, field offsets). Parse these to generate correct SAW type allocations. Currently MIR types are returned as Opaque. Need to map ty::Adt::HASH references to their definitions and emit llvm_struct_type or mir_adt with correct fields.

title: MIR JSON: parse enum variants and generate discriminant constraints
description: mir-json linked-mir.json contains full enum definitions with variant names, discriminant values, and field types. Parse these to generate llvm_precond/mir_precond constraints that restrict discriminants to valid values. Covers Option (0=None, 1=Some), Result (0=Ok, 1=Err), and custom enums like LatchableKey, KeyOperationError, MspState. Currently only handles primitive types.

title: Clang AST: parse SAL annotations from MSVC source
description: SAL annotations (_In_, _Out_, _Inout_, _In_reads_(n), _Out_writes_(n), _Ret_maybenull_, _Must_inspect_result_) are not in LLVM IR. clang -ast-dump exposes them as AnnotateAttr nodes when using clang's SAL emulation headers. Current parse_sal_annotation handles basic cases but needs testing with real MSVC SAL headers and edge cases like _In_opt_, _Inout_updates_bytes_, _Post_satisfies_.

title: Clang AST: handle class/struct field types and sizes
description: parse_cpp_type currently returns Opaque for any non-primitive type. Need to resolve class/struct types by walking the AST to find RecordDecl nodes, extracting field names, types, and byte offsets. This enables generating llvm_alias or llvm_struct_type in SAW specs with correct sizes. Key for MSVC std::string (SSO layout), HeaderMap, Request, etc.

title: Restore unknown_impl &dyn Trait e2e test with real tool support
description: Removed the trait_unknown_impl e2e case (tests/e2e/cases/03-rust-trait-dispatch/unknown_impl/) on 2026-05-29 because it relied on a hand-rolled SAW driver (run_unknown_impl.ps1) and a duplicated trait_schema.json that re-stated the trait definition already present in the .rs source. That gave false confidence: the verifier path being exercised was bespoke, not the production verify-rust.ps1 path. Restore once saw-spec-gen can drive the full flow end-to-end from .rs source alone.
design: Add src/parsers/rust_source.rs (lightweight regex-based parser, scoped to pub trait + pub fn forms; reject unsupported types) producing TraitSchema and FnSig. Extend gen-rust-trait-stubs with --rust-file (mutually exclusive with --schema). Teach verify-rust.ps1 a dyn-trait branch that relaxes the integer-only signature check, calls llvm-as on the generated stubs.ll, llvm-link's the bitcode, and emits a driver using llvm_global '__stubvtable_<Trait>' for the vtable arg.
acceptance_criteria: 1. saw-spec-gen gen-rust-trait-stubs accepts --rust-file <foo.rs> and derives the TraitSchema in-process (no JSON intermediate, no hand-written schema). 2. verify-rust.ps1 detects '&dyn TraitName' parameters in the target fn signature, invokes the stub generator, links the produced .ll into the bitcode, and emits a SAW driver that allocates fat-pointer args (data ptr + vtable global) and passes trait_overrides to llvm_verify. 3. The two add_step cases (verified -> VERIFIED, disproved -> DISPROVED) round-trip via Runner='rust' in cases.psd1 -- no custom Script entry. 4. trait_schema.json and run_unknown_impl.ps1 are not reintroduced.

title: Restore async Rust e2e test once verify-rust.ps1 supports coroutine resume functions
description: The async_rust e2e case (formerly tests/e2e/cases/06-async-rust/add_one_coroutine/) was removed in commit <ADDED-IN-COMMIT> because run_async_test.ps1 was a custom runner whose existence hid a real tool gap: verify-rust.ps1 only supports plain (iN, ...) -> iN signatures, so async fn add_one(x: u32) -> u32 (which rustc lowers to (sret([12 x i8]), i32) -> void with the actual arithmetic in a separately-emitted coroutine resume symbol) cannot be verified through the standard pipeline.
design: Pieces the deleted runner had to know that the standard wrapper does not: (1) coroutine state layout — for add_one(x: u32) it is { i32 x; i32 polled; i8 tag } (12 bytes, align 4); (2) symbol mangling for the resume function — _RNCNvCs<hash>_<crate>7add_one0<...>; (3) Poll<u32> return ABI — { i32 disc, i32 val } where Ready = (0, v); (4) opaque task context allocation. Steps 1 and 3 generalise from rustc's coroutine lowering pass; step 2 is a regex sweep over llvm-dis output. None of this is async-specific to add_one — it should live in saw-spec-gen so any async fn with integer args/return Just Works.
acceptance_criteria: verify-rust.ps1 (or a dedicated subcommand) detects async fn / coroutine lowering, resolves the resume symbol, sets up the coroutine state struct, and verifies it against the Cryptol spec WITHOUT a per-test bespoke .ps1 driver. Then restore the test under tests/e2e/cases/06-async-rust/ with Runner='rust' in cases.psd1.

title: Document and version-stamp result.json schema
description:
The per-run `result.json` file written by verify.ps1 / verify-rust.ps1 / verify-equiv.ps1 is currently defined inline in each wrapper (verify.ps1:283-296, verify-rust.ps1:395-408) and read by verify-equiv.ps1:110-118 with no schema document. The pretty-specs adapter (saw-spec-gen-3eq + pretty-specs-2oa) needs a stable contract.

Work:
- Add `schema_version: "1"` field to every result.json payload.
- Document the schema in tests/e2e/README.md (or a new `docs/result-json.md`): fields `side` (cpp|rust|equiv), `function`, `cryptol_fn`, `verdict` (VERIFIED|DISPROVED|UNKNOWN|EQUIVALENT|NOT EQUIVALENT), `counterexample` (array of {name, value, bits}), `expected`, `actual`, plus optional `solver`, `time_secs`, `impl_file`.
- Factor the writer into a single PowerShell helper module (e.g. scripts/Write-ResultJson.ps1) sourced by all three verify scripts so the shape stays in sync.
- Optional: emit a JSON Schema (.beads/schemas/result.v1.json or similar) and add a tiny test that validates one fixture.

Acceptance:
- All three verify scripts produce result.json files with a `schema_version` field.
- Schema doc exists and is linked from README.md.
- The collect-results subcommand (saw-spec-gen-3eq) rejects unknown schema versions with a clear error.

title: Add collect-results subcommand: aggregate result.json into pretty-specs proof manifest
description:
Add a collect-results subcommand to saw-spec-gen that walks a directory tree, finds every out_*/result.json produced by verify.ps1 / verify-rust.ps1 / verify-equiv.ps1, and emits a single proof_manifest.json in the format pretty-specs (../pretty-specs) consumes via its --proof-status <FILE> flag.

This is the saw-spec-gen-side half of the closed-loop "verified badges in docs" integration. Pairs with pretty-specs-2oa (which proposes the same adapter — by putting it here we avoid coupling pretty-specs to our result.json shape).

Inputs:
- `--root <DIR>` (required): directory to scan recursively for `result.json`
- `--output <FILE>` (default `proof_manifest.json`): manifest path
- `--cryptol-fn-map <FILE>` (optional): JSON map of impl-fn -> Cryptol-fn aliases when names differ
- `--format flat|structured` (default `flat` until pretty-specs-ua2 lands)

Behavior:
- Skip `out_*` dirs with no `result.json` (verification didn't run yet).
- Use the verdict mapping: VERIFIED -> proven; DISPROVED -> failed (reason from counterexample); UNKNOWN -> not_attempted; EQUIVALENT/NOT EQUIVALENT -> proven/failed with source=equiv.
- Key entries by `function` field from result.json. When --cryptol-fn-map applies, key by Cryptol-fn instead so pretty-specs matches by Item::Function.name.

Acceptance:
- `saw-spec-gen collect-results --root tests/e2e/cases --output manifest.json` produces a manifest covering every case the e2e suite ran.
- Round-trip: feed manifest to pretty-specs and the function pages render badges (once pretty-specs-8yu + pretty-specs-354 land).
- Unit test against fixture result.json files.

title: OOM on large AST files -- need streaming parser or helpful error
description: Large clang AST dumps (100MB+) cause OOM when read_to_string + serde_json::from_str loads the entire file. Either implement a streaming parser that processes nodes incrementally, or at minimum detect file size and emit a helpful error with suggestions to use -ast-dump-filter or split the input.
close_reason: Added file size guards (100MB for AST/MIR, 200MB for LLVM IR) in parse_ast, parse_mir, and parse_llvm_ir. On oversized files, emits actionable error with suggestions (use -ast-dump-filter, opt --passes=internalize, jq pre-filtering). 3 new tests.

title: Handle concatenated JSON objects from clang -ast-dump-filter
description: clang -ast-dump=json -ast-dump-filter=FuncName emits multiple top-level JSON objects concatenated (not a JSON array). serde_json::from_str fails on this. Need to detect and handle multiple concatenated JSON objects by splitting on top-level braces or using a streaming deserializer.
close_reason: parse_ast now handles concatenated JSON objects from clang -ast-dump-filter. Falls back to brace-depth splitting when standard parse fails. Wraps multiple objects in a synthetic TranslationUnitDecl. String-aware splitter handles nested braces in JSON values. 5 new tests.

title: Model sret (struct return) calling convention for functions returning large types
description: When LLVM lowers a function returning a large struct, it transforms the return into an sret pointer parameter. The from-llvm-ir parser needs to detect sret attributes and model the function as taking an output pointer parameter (AllocMutable) instead of having a return value.
close_reason: Implemented sret detection: finds sret attribute on parameters, models as writeonly output pointer, sets return type to void. Handles nested parens in sret(type). 3 new tests.

title: Resolve C++ class/STL type sizes instead of emitting zero-size opaque types
description: When parse_cpp_type encounters unknown types like std::string, std::vector, or user classes not in struct_map, it emits Opaque { size_bytes: 0 }. Add a well-known STL type size table for MSVC x64 (std::string=32, std::vector=24, etc.) and also compute sizes from struct field layouts.
close_reason: Added STL type size table (MSVC x64 ABI) covering string, vector, map, shared_ptr, unique_ptr, mutex, function, span, string_view, plus Win32 types (GUID, CRITICAL_SECTION, etc). Also computes struct sizes from field layouts with proper alignment padding. 9 new tests.

title: Implement from-llvm-ir subcommand for rich type layout info
description: Parse struct type definitions (%struct.Name = type { ... }) from LLVM IR .ll files, compute their sizes, and produce TypeInfo::Struct with fields and size_bytes instead of zero-size Opaque types. This lets generated SAW specs use llvm_array N (llvm_int 8) for proper allocation sizes.
close_reason: Implemented struct type definition parsing from LLVM IR. Computes sizes with padding and alignment for normal and packed structs. 7 new tests.

title: Generate Cryptol type constraint functions alongside SAW specs
description: In addition to .saw files, optionally generate .cry (Cryptol) files with type constraint functions. E.g., for Result<T,E> generate a Cryptol function that constrains discriminant to {0,1}. These can be imported into existing Cryptol specs to add type-system-derived bounds. Add --emit-cryptol flag.

title: MIR JSON: handle async function coroutine types
description: Async Rust functions in mir-json produce TyCoroutine types with discriminant states and upvar fields. Generate mir_coroutine_value-based specs that constrain the coroutine state to valid discriminant values and properly type the captured variables. Needed for verifying async KeyExchangeSession methods.

title: Implement from-llvm-ir subcommand for language-agnostic attribute extraction
description: Parse LLVM IR text (.ll files from llvm-dis) to extract function attributes: readonly, writeonly, noalias, nocapture, nounwind, nonnull, dereferenceable(N), noundef, align(N), range. This is language-agnostic and works for any language compiled to LLVM (C, C++, Rust, Swift, etc.). Less rich than clang AST or MIR but requires no source-level tooling.

title: Handle noexcept and exception specifications
description: C++ noexcept functions cannot throw. The clang AST encodes this in the function type (noexcept qualifier). Generate can_throw=false for noexcept functions. For non-noexcept functions, emit a comment warning that exception paths are possible. Future: integrate with SAW exception-lowering pass to generate specs that model both success and throw paths.

title: Handle C++ template/generic functions in clang AST
description: clang AST includes FunctionTemplateDecl for template functions. These have type parameters (T, U) that need to be resolved or flagged as unresolvable. For MSP C++ this matters for std:: functions like std::find, std::transform. Generate specs that are parameterized or flag as needing manual instantiation.

title: Emit type metadata JSON to support pretty-specs type cross-check
Batch verify mode: consume pretty-specs --emit-function-list output
description:
Today every verify run requires explicit `--cryptol-fn` and `--function` pairs. With pretty-specs-k1g (`--emit-function-list`), pretty-specs will emit a JSON array of {name, signature, arity} for every Item::Function in a parsed .cry spec. saw-spec-gen should be able to consume that and iterate.

Add either:
- A new `--spec-manifest <FILE>` flag on `gen-verify` that reads the function list and runs gen-verify once per function, OR
- A wrapper subcommand `verify-all --spec-manifest <FILE> --cpp <DIR> --rust <DIR>` that orchestrates verify.ps1 / verify-rust.ps1 invocations and writes a consolidated output dir.

The wrapper approach is preferred because it composes cleanly with collect-results (saw-spec-gen-3eq) for the full pipeline:

  pretty-specs SDEP.cry --emit-function-list > fns.json
  saw-spec-gen verify-all --spec-manifest fns.json --cpp impl/ --output runs/
  saw-spec-gen collect-results --root runs/ --output manifest.json
  pretty-specs SDEP.cry -o docs/ --proof-status manifest.json

Acceptance:
- One command discovers every Item::Function in a .cry spec and attempts verification for each.
- Functions with no implementation get logged but don't fail the run.
- Output dir is laid out so collect-results can find each run's result.json.
